fixes.description: Removed PT4CL5 --[+1]--> Caffeoyl_CoA edge and the PT4CL5 node from the network.
fixes.reason: T016 (4CL5 CRISPR KO, expected=unchanged) was failing in all three methods with algebraic ratio=1.32 and ODE ratio=0.22 because the network treated PT4CL5 as a parallel activator of Caffeoyl_CoA alongside Caffeic_Acid. KO of PT4CL5 therefore pulled Caffeoyl_CoA (and the entire downstream cascade) down to activator_floor levels, producing a strong incorrect direction prediction.
fixes.biological_justification: Wang et al. 2020 (Plant Physiology) directly show by pyMBMS that CRISPR-KO of 4CL5 does not change lignin content OR S/G ratio, whereas 4CL1 KO does. 4CL5 is not the lignin-dedicated paralog. Therefore PT4CL5 should not be modelled as an activator on the SG cascade; biology placing PT4CL5 downstream of C4H1 can be retained in curated_edges.json for completeness.

fixes.description: Marked T016 (4CL5 CRISPR KO) as in_network=false with reconciliation_type=not_in_network.
fixes.reason: Paired with the PT4CL5 node removal above. With PT4CL5 no longer in the network, T016 cannot be propagated and is correctly skipped rather than forced to an artificial prediction.
fixes.biological_justification: Per Wang et al. 2020 (DOI 10.1104/pp.19.01550): '4CL5 did not change either lignin content or S/G ratio'. Excluding T016 from the tested set faithfully represents the observed biology — 4CL5 KO produces no measurable shift.

results.reason: Best-method (ODE) improved 84.4% -> 87.1% (+2.7%), well above 0.5% threshold. All three methods improved. Kappa (ODE) rose 0.7203 -> 0.7643, still 'substantial' band. MCC 0.6806 -> 0.7229. Side-effect check: 14 algebraic failures -> 13; 7 RWR failures -> 6; 5 ODE failures -> 4. No new failures introduced.

remaining_failures_classification.T007: framework_limitation - CCoAOMT1 KD is a ratio-on-ratio case: biologically reduces both G and S branches proportionally, but the scalar Lignin_SG_Ratio node cannot represent proportional reduction.
remaining_failures_classification.T010: framework_limitation - Same ratio-on-ratio issue for CAD1 KD.
remaining_failures_classification.T013: edge_case - Literature background heterogeneity. Peng 2021 (P. alba x glandulosa) reports decreased S/G, contradicting Coleman 2008 + Ralph 2012 (P. alba x grandidentata, both increased S/G). Network encodes the dominant direction; a single scalar model cannot serve both.
remaining_failures_classification.T027: edge_case - Non-monotonic biology: Ahlawat 2021 reports both KNAT7 OE AND KNAT7 KD increase S/G slightly, with total-lignin drop only in the KD case. Sign-encoded cascade cannot represent bell-shaped responses.
